title: Repsol Surpasses 1,500 Service Stations in Spain and Portugal Offering 100% Renewable Nexa Diesel
content: Repsol has reached a new milestone in its strategy to decarbonize transport by surpassing 1,500 service stations â€“ 1,429 in Spain and 71 in Portugal â€“ supplying Nexa Diesel of 100% renewable origin. This step forward reaffirms the company's commitment to renewable fuels and expands the range of technologies made available to support customersâ€™ mobility, thus, meeting the objective outlined in the update of the companyâ€™s 2024-2027 strategic plan. With this achievement, Repsol consolidates its position as the most relevant 100% renewable fuel networks in Europe, reaching more than 210 million liters sold so far this year. Currently, more than 40% of the companyâ€™s network of service stations offers renewable fuel, demonstrating that decarbonizing transport with renewable liquid fuels is a viable solution for combustion engine vehicles. These vehicles today represent 97% of the Spanish and European vehicle fleet, and 87% of sales in Spain so far this year.
author: Analysts Account
